event: Admitted to the obstetric department for pre-term labor
Received 10 mg of nifedipine 4 times in 1 h
Pulmonary edema and hypoxemia
Transfer to the intensive care unit (ICU)
Blood pressure was 101/61 mm Hg
ECG revealed a sinus tachycardia (142 bpm)
Oxygen saturation was 98% under 100% high flow oxygenation
Physical examination revealed a loud first heart sound
Physical examination revealed a diastolic murmur
Physical examination revealed bilateral wheezing
Physical examination revealed jugular venous distension
Fall in systolic blood pressure
Worsening desaturation (78%)
Metabolic acidosis
Hyperlactatemia (34 mg/dL)
Intubated and ventilated
Chest X-ray confirmed pulmonary edema
Cardiac echography revealed unknown severe mitral stenosis
Cardiac echography revealed normal left ventricular function
Cardiac echography revealed enlargement and pressure overload of the left atrium
Cardiac echography revealed severe pulmonary hypertension
Loop diuretics (furosemide) and full-dose heparin were administered
Ultrasound examination showed no fetal distress
Transoesophageal echocardiography showed a severe rheumatic mitral stenosis
Spontaneous vaginal delivery occurred
Baby was alive and transferred to intensive neonatal care unit
Mother remained in a critical state with hypotension
Endometritis with sepsis
Treated by ceftriaxone and ampicillin
Percutaneous double balloon mitral valvuloplasty was performed
Mitral mean gradient was reduced from 10 to 3 mmHg
Mitral area was increased from 0.5 to 1.65 cm2
Pulmonary pressure was normalized
Recovery was rapid
Patient could be extubated
